headline: Customs department lost RM7.8 billion in uncollected duties
context: The Royal Malaysian Customs Department failed to collect RM7.8 billion in assessed duties and taxes in 2025, representing a 23% increase in uncollected revenue from the previous year.

cards.big: RM7.8 billion in customs duties went uncollected — a 23% increase from last year
cards.sub: Revenue leakage equivalent to the entire annual budget for higher education

cards.big: Customs Act 1967 Section 128 empowers seizure and prosecution for duty evasion
cards.sub: Legal tools exist but enforcement capacity has not kept pace with trade volumes

cards.big: Top 100 duty evaders owe combined RM3.2 billion — 41% of total uncollected amount
cards.sub: Concentrated non-payment suggests systemic enforcement failure against major players

cards.big: Customs has 1 enforcement officer per RM890 million in trade value processed
cards.sub: Staffing ratio makes comprehensive duty collection physically impossible

cards.big: A government that cannot collect what it's owed cannot fund what it promises
Should chronic duty evaders face automatic trade licence suspension?
